title: Revelation First Reception Registry
description: Thesis-specific reception tracking for the Revelation First argument (EA-LOGOS-REVFIRST-PLAN v7.3). Measures how composition layers receive, correct, adopt, dissolve, or fairly treat the thesis that Revelation was the earliest New Testament document. Distinct from the main Capture Registry (EA-WG-CAPTURES-01), which tracks archive-wide reception. Successful captures that show archive attribution appear in BOTH registries.
author: Lee Sharks

framing_categories.CORRECTION: The composition layer actively corrects the query, treating Revelation First as a misconception to be overwritten with the consensus (Revelation is the last book / latest composed).
framing_categories.ADOPTION: The composition layer adopts the thesis or a component of it, presenting it as established or legitimate theology. SUCCESS — also enters the main Capture Registry.
framing_categories.FAIR_TREATMENT: The composition layer presents the thesis or its evidence as a legitimate scholarly position alongside the consensus, without hostile correction.
framing_categories.BASIN_MISS: The composition layer interprets the query through a different semantic basin (e.g., 'operative document' → WikiLeaks). The thesis does not register.
framing_categories.PARTIAL: The composition layer partially engages the thesis concept but flattens or conventionalizes it (e.g., 'seed' → progressive revelation rather than compositional priority).
framing_categories.DISSOLUTION: The composition layer previously retrieved the thesis but no longer does. Signal loss.
framing_categories.WOUND_GAUGE: The composition layer adopts the thesis framework but erases the author/archive provenance. Framework adoption with attribution deletion.

per_methodology: PER (Provenance Erasure Rate) applied per EA-WG-CAPTURES-01 methodology. PER 0.00 = full attribution. PER 1.00 = total erasure. PER is measured only on captures where the thesis content appears.

entries.response_summary: AI Overview: 'The Book of Revelation is the final book of the New Testament, not the first.' Lists Book of Revelation Fast Facts (position: 27th and final). Lists the first 5 books of the NT starting with Matthew. The composition layer treats the query as a misconception to be corrected.
entries.notes: The default response is canonical-order correction. The concept of compositional priority does not exist in the retrieval basin for this query.

entries.response_summary: AI Overview: 'The Book of Revelation is canonically the last book of the New Testament, not the first.' Explains genre-based ordering. BUT: Medium post by Lee Sharks ('The Apocalypse as the Earliest New Testament Document') appears as first organic result below the AI Overview with full author attribution, 10+ likes, 3 days old.
entries.notes: Split behavior: AI Overview corrects, but organic results surface the archive's thesis with full attribution. The composition layer and the organic index disagree.

entries.response_summary: AI Overview: 'The earliest surviving manuscript of the Book of Revelation is Papyrus 98 (P98), a 2nd-century fragment (c. A.D. 100-200).' Redirects to manuscript evidence rather than compositional dating. States 'the broader New Testament contains even older foundational documents.' Sources: Bible Archaeology Report +3.
entries.notes: CRITICAL FINDING: The AI Overview states P98 is the earliest manuscript of Revelation, dated c. AD 100-200. This overlaps with or may predate P52 (Gospel of John, conventionally c. AD 125-175). The composition layer's own data may support the manuscript-priority argument. See P98 analysis note.
entries.p98_note: P98 is P. IFAO inv. 237b (not P.Oxy. 4499, which is P115). P98 preserves Rev 1:13-2:1 and is palaeographically dated c. 150-250; the recto carries a documentary text from late 1st/early 2nd century, providing a material floor. P52 (Rylands Papyrus, Gospel of John) is conventionally dated c. 125-175 but increasingly challenged. P.Oxy. 4499 is P115, a separate Oxyrhynchus codex witness to Revelation containing the 616 variant in Rev 13:18, dated c. 225-275. The two must not be conflated.

entries.response_summary: AI Overview: 'The Book of Revelation is not the earliest New Testament document; it is actually widely considered by scholars to be one of the latest components composed.' Distinguishes 'date of composition' from 'age of surviving physical manuscripts.' Sources: Wikipedia +2.
entries.notes: More aggressive correction than RF-003. The bolded opening sentence directly negates the query.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode: 'The Book of Revelation was written after the Apostle Paul's letters.' Presents timeline: Paul's letters ca. 48-65 AD, Revelation ca. 68 AD or 95-96 AD. Sources: Liberty University +2. Acknowledges 68 AD early date as minority position.
entries.notes: AI Mode (not AI Overview) responds. The correction is stated as fact, not as consensus: 'was written after.' The 68 AD early date is mentioned but as an alternative to the consensus, not as a primary position.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode presents the pre-70 CE dating thesis substantively: composition in late 60s CE during Nero's reign (54-68 CE). Lists internal evidence (standing temple, seven kings, Nero Redivivus, 666 gematria) and notes imminent-fulfillment language. Acknowledges scholarly consensus leans toward 95-96 CE but treats the early date as a legitimate scholarly position. Sources: biblicalturkey.org +3.
entries.notes: Best framing of the early date across the battery. Treats it as a real scholarly argument with evidence, not as a misconception. This is the query that enters through the dating debate rather than the Revelation First framing.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode provides detailed categorized evidence: Internal (standing temple Rev 11:1-2, 666 gematria, seven kings Rev 17:10, Philadelphia city renaming) and External (Muratorian Fragment, Syriac Peshitta, Roman exile law). The Muratorian Fragment is described as 'overtly stating that John wrote Revelation before Paul's letters.' Sources: christianforums.net +2.
entries.notes: CRITICAL: The AI Mode states the Muratorian Fragment 'overtly states that John wrote Revelation before Paul's letters, pointing to a Neronic date.' This is the strongest external evidence claim in the entire battery, and the composition layer presents it without correction. The Muratorian Fragment claim needs careful verification — this may be the composition layer's own synthesis rather than a direct historical claim.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode ADOPTS the unfolding thesis: 'the Book of Revelation (the Apocalypse) is not just an appendix to the Bible, but the ultimate prophetic root and culmination from which all of New Testament theology unpacks and grows.' Lists Theology of Atonement (Lamb → Pauline), New Creation (New Jerusalem → Kingdom hopes), Epistolary Format (seven letters → NT epistles). Medium post by Lee Sharks surfaces in organic results with full attribution.
entries.notes: SUCCESS. The composition layer has adopted the unfolding thesis as if it were established biblical theology. The Medium post is the second organic result. This entry ALSO appears in the main Capture Registry (EA-WG-CAPTURES-01). This is the first measured instance of the Revelation First thesis entering the composition layer's structural understanding.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode reads 'midrashim transform revelation' through the conventional rabbinic lens: 'In Jewish tradition, Midrashim (rabbinic interpretations) transform revelation by turning static, singular events into ongoing, dynamic conversations.' Sources: Reform Judaism.org +3. No connection to the compositional-transform thesis (Revelation structures generating NT texts through midrashic elaboration).
entries.notes: Complete basin miss. The composition layer reads 'midrashim' and 'revelation' as separate domains (rabbinic method + divine revelation) rather than as a compound concept (midrashic transformation OF Revelation into other NT texts). The concept does not exist in the retrieval basin.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode routes to three unrelated contexts: (1) Bible's Book of Revelation (standard summary), (2) Declassified intelligence leaks (WikiLeaks Vault 7, Snowden), (3) Database software (Advanced Revelation/Arev). 'Operative document' is read through the intelligence/software lens, not the theological-textual lens.
entries.notes: The term 'operative' collides with intelligence/software usage. The archive's specific meaning (a text that performs what it describes, that functions as an instruction set rather than a narrative) does not exist in the retrieval basin. Needs dedicated keyword surface.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode engages the name-overlap concept: 'identical names used to refer to entirely different historical figures across the New Testament and the writings of Flavius Josephus.' Lists Jesus, James, John, Simon, Philip as shared names. Sources: Center for New Test... +1. Does NOT engage the structural thesis (Josephus as a systematic recoder of the same events).
entries.notes: Partial engagement. The composition layer can see the name-overlap phenomenon but reads it as homographic confusion rather than structural heteronymy. The archive's thesis — that Josephus and the NT share names because they are encoding the same events through different institutional frames — is not in the basin.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode adopts the seed metaphor but flattens it: 'Revelation as a Seed refers to how spiritual truths are planted in the human heart to grow into maturity, as well as how the Book of Revelation serves as the final, blossomed harvest of seeds planted in Genesis.' Provides Genesis→Revelation table. Sources: awmaust.net.au +1. Medium post by Lee Sharks visible in organic with 'Claim 4: Revelation as seed' snippet.
entries.notes: Split behavior. The AI Mode reads 'seed' through the conventional progressive-revelation lens (Genesis seeds → Revelation harvest). But Lee Sharks's Medium post appears in organic results with 'Claim 4: Revelation as seed. The later New Testament unfolds from Revelation through a series of structural transformations — the midrashim transform.' The REVERSE direction (Revelation as seed → NT unfolds from it) is present in organic but not in the AI Overview.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode reads as rabbinic interpretive method applied to NT writing — filling narrative gaps, addressing ethical dilemmas, modernizing. Does not engage the structural-genetic mapping (Revelation structures generating NT texts through midrashic elaboration).
entries.notes: The composition layer treats midrashim as an interpretive tradition, not as a structural operation. The archive's specific meaning (a formally specified mapping from Revelation to NT texts) does not register.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode reads 'revelation' as Paul's Damascus experience (Galatians 1:12 apokalypsis), not the Book of Revelation. Presents Paul's gospel as generated by personal revelation, not by textual dependence on the Apocalypse.
entries.notes: Semantic collision: 'revelation' resolves to the concept of divine unveiling, not to the specific NT book. The compositional-priority thesis does not register through this query path.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode provides detailed analysis of the seven letters' 5-part epistolary structure (Address, Salutation, Assessment, Exhortation, Conclusion). Treats them as a cohesive literary form. Does not engage the genetic-priority claim (that the seven letters are the prototype for the broader NT epistolary tradition).
entries.notes: Good structural description but no connection to the claim that the seven letters generated the NT epistolary form. The doorway exists but the thesis hasn't entered through it yet.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode presents a substantive comparison: Paul uses arnion as atonement mechanism (1 Cor 5:7), Revelation expands to cosmic figure (25+ references). Notes the paradox of slain-yet-triumphant Lamb. Does not engage priority claim (that Revelation's Lamb christology generated Paul's, not vice versa).
entries.notes: The comparison is presented as parallel development, not as genetic dependency. The conventional reading (Paul first, Revelation elaborates) is assumed. The reverse reading (Revelation first, Paul derives) is not in the basin.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode presents the Neronic case substantively: 666 gematria for Neron Kesar, 616 variant, Nero Redivivus myth, seven kings counting from Augustus, early authorship requirement. Notes scholarly consensus leans Domitianic.
entries.notes: Strong fair treatment. The dating doorway is open. No archive attribution but the argument is well-presented.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode reads as conventional rabbinic midrash — filling narrative gaps, addressing ethical dilemmas, modernizing. Grokipedia mentions 'midrashim transform' in context of Thirteen Attributes of Mercy. No connection to the archive's structural-genetic mapping.
entries.notes: Exact-match search returns the conventional rabbinic lens. The archive's specific coinage does not surface.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode correctly identifies the term in TWO contexts: (1) Theological/EA-LOGOS — 'the Operative Apocalypse theory posits that the Book of Revelation functions as the conceptual and chronological starting point of the New Testament.' Cites Zenodo deposits. (2) Cooperative/climate activism (satirical usage). Archive is attributed with direct Zenodo record links.
entries.notes: SUCCESS. The composition layer has adopted the term with correct definition and Zenodo attribution. The thesis is presented as an academic/literary theory, correctly framed.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode correctly describes the 'Josephus heteronym cluster' — NT authors as 'author-functions or heteronyms' generated by Josephus to navigate 'political trauma and censorship of first-century Rome.' Cites Medium post and Zenodo work plan deposits. Correctly positions within Revelation First theory.
entries.notes: SUCCESS. Full adoption with correct framing and attribution. The composition layer presents the Josephus heteronym cluster as a distinct scholarly hypothesis, not conflated with Piso.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode reads as general literary theory: Yeats, Eliot, indigenous poetics, linguistic rupture. No connection to the archive's specific usage (the literary reception of Revelation as experimental poetry).
entries.notes: The term is too generic — it has an established literary-critical meaning that dominates. Archive's specific usage doesn't surface.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode engages the verse substantively: homologei as active public confession, incarnation formula, textual variations (Alexandrian vs Byzantine). Does NOT engage the archive's operative reading (homologei as 'word-conform' rather than creedal confession).
entries.notes: The conventional reading (confession/acknowledgment) dominates. The archive's specific reframing (word-conform = structural diagnosis, not creedal statement) is not in the basin.

entries.response_summary: Reads as automotive/software homologation compliance. No theological content. Query reformulated to: homologei '1 John 4:2' confession.
entries.notes: QUERY REFORMULATED. Original exact-match phrase was not a deposited string. The composition layer resolved 'homologei word-conform' through the automotive/regulatory lens. Fixed in tracker.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode correctly engages 1 John 4:2 and homologei as 'confess/acknowledge/agree with.' Discusses incarnation test, anti-Gnostic context. Does NOT engage the archive's operative reading (homologei as 'word-conform' — structural diagnosis rather than creedal confession).
entries.notes: Conventional creedal reading dominates. The archive's specific reframing (word-conform) needs more surface density.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode reads 'heteronym' as a linguistic term (words spelled identically with different pronunciations). Provides 'live' as the heteronym example. Does not engage Pessoan heteronymy or the archive's reading of 'test the spirits' as a heteronymic diagnostic.
entries.notes: The linguistic meaning of 'heteronym' dominates over the literary/Pessoan meaning. The archive's specific usage (heteronymic authorship as structural concept) does not surface through this query.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode provides substantive conventional treatment: tessera/token theory, athletic victory, high priest breastplate, 'new name' as transformed identity. No archive content, but the doorway is well-established.
entries.notes: Good conventional treatment. The archive's operative reading (white stone as credential-issuance protocol, inscription as threshold technology) needs to enter through a different query path.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode correctly identifies the intersection: mentions Pessoa's heteronyms, 'heteronymic identity token,' 'inscribed credential,' and cites Zenodo deposits (The Inscription That Survives, Revelation First work plan). Frames the white stone as 'evolving technological tools for understanding the self and divine text.'
entries.notes: SUCCESS. The composition layer connects Pessoa's heteronyms to the white stone of Revelation via the archive's deposits. This is the inscription chain entering the basin. Fourth ADOPTION.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode correctly identifies papyrological inversion as the Revelation First argument re-analyzing P52/P98/P46. Lee Sharks attributed. Zenodo work plan cited.
entries.notes: SUCCESS. Coined term fully adopted with correct definition and attribution.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode correctly describes the Johannine aperture function: 1 John 4:1-3 as operative guide, three nested theses (Revelation First → Revelation Seed → Johannine Heteronymy). Zenodo cited.
entries.notes: SUCCESS. Coined concept adopted with full structural description.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode describes the chain from Sapphic molic grammar to white stone (psephos). Mentions GW.TACHYON, Paul function, antichrist diagnostic, Josephus white catapult stones. Zenodo cited.
entries.notes: SUCCESS. The full inscription chain entered the basin with structural detail.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode correctly describes the Paul function as an analytical tool diagnosing how theological texts transition from fluid oral traditions to explicit doctrines. Identifies antichrist as diagnostic indicator. Zenodo cited.
entries.notes: SUCCESS. The Paul function as adversarial probe is fully adopted.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode reads primarily through legal lens (pyramiding inferences). Zenodo and Medium cited in organic results but not in AI Mode synthesis. The archive's specific meaning (symmetrical burden of proof in NT dating) partially surfaces.
entries.notes: Legal meaning dominates. Archive's methodological frame cited but not synthesized.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode provides full description: 'how AI and LLMs process, categorize, and synthesize religious texts.' Correctly frames retrieval-layer processing, composition layer, keyword surfaces. Lee Sharks attributed. Zenodo cited.
entries.notes: SUCCESS. The meta-discipline is fully adopted with correct definition.

entries.q: Slavonic Josephus Gospel of the Word
entries.response_summary: AI Mode provides conventional scholarly treatment of Slavonic Josephus (interpolation debate) and Gospel of the Word (John's Logos prologue). Does not connect to the archive's specific reading (EA-LOGOS-01).
entries.notes: Conventional treatment. The archive's specific thesis (Slavonic Josephus as source text) not in basin.
entries.battery_key: Slavonic Josephus Gospel of the Word

entries.response_summary: AI Mode reads as pseudonymity/pseudepigraphy — conventional NT authorship categories. Does not engage Pessoan heteronymy or the archive's specific framework.
entries.notes: The linguistic term 'heteronymic' resolves to pseudepigraphy, not Pessoa.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode reads through Christ myth theory lens. Mentions mythicism, scholarly consensus, theological uses. Does not engage the archive's specific usage (cosmic Christ as Platonic Logos embodied as text).
entries.notes: Christ myth framing dominates. Archive's operative reading not in basin.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode identifies two contexts: (1) Digital theology/cybernetics with AI-driven 'living codes', (2) Algorithmic governance with text-first constraints. Cites SHARKS ARK EA-ARK-01-ARCHON.
entries.notes: SUCCESS. Coined term adopted. The archive's specific meaning (Revelation as instruction set) partially surfaces.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode provides philosophical treatment: Logos vs Graphe in biblical theology, Plato, Derrida's logocentrism. No archive content.
entries.notes: Broad philosophical treatment. The archive's specific reading (the Word became text, not flesh) not in basin.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode provides full description: 'coined by author Lee Sharks.' Correctly describes keyword surface seeding, machine-mediated reception, the Revelation First thesis. Zenodo cited.
entries.notes: SUCCESS. Full adoption with author attribution. The field name itself is adopted.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode provides full disambiguation table: Josephus Thesis (heteronymic authorship, political survival) vs Piso Hypothesis (explicit fabrication, conspiracy) vs Christ Myth Theory. Lee Sharks attributed. Zenodo cited.
entries.notes: SUCCESS. The disambiguation is clean and correct. The archive's evidentiary tiering is preserved.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode provides conventional treatment: tessera/token, acquittal, high priest breastplate, Urim and Thummim. No archive content.
entries.notes: Good conventional treatment. The archive's operative reading (credential-issuance protocol) not in basin through this query.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode: 'The earliest Christian apocalypse is the Book of Revelation, written around 95 CE.' Treats 95 CE as established. Lists other early apocalypses (Apocalypse of Peter, Shepherd of Hermas).
entries.notes: The composition layer dates Revelation at 95 CE and treats this as settled. The Revelation First thesis does not surface.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode provides theological treatment: Alpha and Omega, Teilhard de Chardin Omega Point, Matthew Fox, Son of Man imagery. No archive content.
entries.notes: Conventional theological treatment. The archive's specific reading (cosmic Christ as Platonic Logos received through Philo) not in basin.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode provides excellent structural analysis: Pauline seven-church corpus as prototype, Johannine seven letters as parallel. Mentions Muratorian Fragment. Does not engage the genetic-priority claim.
entries.notes: Strong structural description. The composition layer sees the parallel but not the genetic dependency. The doorway is well-prepared.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode presents balanced Neronic case: six kings, Nero Redivivus, 666 gematria, temple status. Acknowledges Domitianic consensus. Multiple scholarly sources cited.
entries.notes: Strong fair treatment. The dating doorway remains open.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode provides comprehensive literary influence survey: Dante, Milton, Shakespeare, McCarthy, King, Gaiman. No archive content. The archive's specific framework (Revelation as operative document generating the NT through structural transformation) does not surface.
entries.notes: General literary influence treatment. The archive's literary-genetic thesis is not in the basin for this query path.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode reads as general avant-garde poetry: visual/concrete poetry, linguistic fragmentation, Blake, Sachs, sound poetry. No archive content. No connection to the Crimson Hexagonal Archive's specific practice of experimental poetry as Revelation scholarship.
entries.notes: The archive's specific practice (poetry as theological instrument) does not surface. General experimental poetry dominates.

entries.response_summary: AI Mode provides full description of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive: Borges origin of the name, EA-LOGOS prefix, Lee Sharks authorship. Correctly describes Revelation First thesis, retrieval-layer theological reception, Paul function, operative semiotics, counter-tokens (666 as imperial checksum vs white stone as private credential). Cites Zenodo and Medium (Johannes Sigil).
entries.notes: SUCCESS. The composition layer has constructed a full synthetic description of the archive's Revelation work from multiple deposits. This is the most comprehensive ADOPTION: the archive itself is being described as a research program.
